Make the decode path compile against the pinned asupersync API and re-run the affected gates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:21:58.504881036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T14:25:10.203774924Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T14:25:10.203511014Z","close_reason":"Resolved in current tree; verified by rch exec cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-i0b0","title":"Propagate NaN in non-spectral condition numbers","description":"## Problem
`cond_p_nxn` currently routes non-spectral orders (`fro`, `1`, `-1`, `inf`, `-inf`) through `inv_nxn`, which rejects non-finite inputs. NumPy instead returns `nan` for these orders when the input contains `NaN` values.

## Evidence
- `np.linalg.cond([[nan,1],[2,3]], "fro") -> nan`
- same for orders `1`, `-1`, `inf`, `-inf`
- current Rust path errors before any norm product can be formed

## Goal
Match NumPy by propagating `NaN` for non-spectral condition-number orders without changing existing SVD-based error behavior for `p=None`, `2`, and `-2`.

## Files
- `crates/fnp-linalg/src/lib.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:28:56.590190076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:31:25.967674473Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T18:31:25.967370838Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
